BRIEF WEAK TORNADO OCCURRED THIS MORNING BASED ON PHOTOGRAPHIC AND VIDEO EVIDENCE IN THE CARSON-COMPTON AREA. CONCENTRATED SHORT PATH OF MINOR DAMAGE TO A COUPLE OF BUILDINGS, VEHICLE DAMAGE FROM DEBRIS, AND TREE DAMAGE. VIDEO TAKEN OF SWIRLING DEBRIS BENEATH UPDRAFT BASE OF THUNDERSTORM. DEGREE OF DAMAGE SUGGESTS 75-MPH EF0 TORNADO.

UPDATES INFORMATION FROM PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 2 WSW COMPTON. THIS WAS THE FIRST OF TWO TORNADOES IN THE LOS ANGELES AREA FROM THIS MORNING. BRIEF WEAK TORNADO OCCURRED THIS MORNING BASED ON PHOTOGRAPHIC AND VIDEO EVIDENCE IN THE CARSON-COMPTON AREA. CONCENTRATED SHORT PATH OF MINOR DAMAGE TO A COUPLE OF BUILDINGS, VEHICLE DAMAGE FROM DEBRIS, AND TREE DAMAGE. DAMAGE OCCURRED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF AVALON BLVD. AND E. WALNUT ST. VIDEO TAKEN OF SWIRLING DEBRIS BENEATH UPDRAFT BASE OF THUNDERSTORM. DEGREE OF DAMAGE SUGGESTS 75-MPH EF0 TORNADO.

A SECOND TORNADO OCCURRED IN THE LOS ANGELES AREA THIS MORNING. A WEAK TORNADO DEVELOPED IN COMPTON NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GREENLEAF BLVD. AND S. SANTA FE AVE. VIDEO AND EYEWITNESS ACCOUNTS SUGGEST THAT THE TORNADO CONTINUED AS FAR EAST AS NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF E. GREENLEAF BLVD. AND E. 69TH ST. THE TORNADO PRODUCED A VERY NARROW PATH OF MINOR DAMAGE INCLUDING A DOWNED POWER LINE, TARPS TORN FROM A NURSERY, MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME, DOWNED SMALL TREE LIMBS, AND SIDING DAMAGE TO A COUPLE OF HOMES. VIDEO OF THE TORNADO SHOWED SWIRLING DEBRIS. THE DAMAGE RATING WAS EF-0, WITH AN ESTIMATED WIND SPEED OF 65 MPH. TORNADO LASTED 856AM-900AM. NOTE, THIS TORNADO WAS SEPARATE FROM, THOUGH NEARBY, THE TORNADO THAT OCCURRED ABOUT 13 MINUTES EARLIER -- FARTHER TO THE WEST TOWARD CARSON.

CORRECTS END-POINT INTERSECTION OF PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 1 SE COMPTON. A SECOND TORNADO OCCURRED IN THE LOS ANGELES AREA THIS MORNING. A WEAK TORNADO DEVELOPED IN COMPTON NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GREENLEAF BLVD. AND S. SANTA FE AVE. VIDEO AND EYEWITNESS ACCOUNTS SUGGEST THAT THE TORNADO CONTINUED AS FAR EAST AS NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF ORCUTT AVE. AND E. 69TH ST. THE TORNADO PRODUCED A VERY NARROW PATH OF MINOR DAMAGE INCLUDING A DOWNED POWER LINE, TARPS TORN FROM A NURSERY, MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME, DOWNED SMALL TREE LIMBS, AND SIDING DAMAGE TO A COUPLE OF HOMES. VIDEO OF THE TORNADO SHOWED SWIRLING DEBRIS. THE DAMAGE RATING WAS EF-0, WITH AN ESTIMATED WIND SPEED OF 65 MPH. TORNADO LASTED 856AM-900AM. NOTE, THIS TORNADO WAS SEPARATE FROM, THOUGH NEARBY, THE TORNADO THAT OCCURRED ABOUT 13 MINUTES EARLIER -- FARTHER TO THE WEST TOWARD CARSON.
